category_title: Low-proficiency international students with high linguistic anxiety and reliance on external support
minimal_context: The student and tutor are reviewing the first draft of an English article to correct mistakes.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's review the first draft of your English article. I have pointed out the mistakes in your grammar and vocabulary that need to be fixed.

STUDENT: My vocabulary is not enough and English grammar is totally different from Chinese grammar. It is hard for me to write a good English article.

TUTOR: What do you think you should do to correct these mistakes?

STUDENT: I realize that my writing skill learned in China cannot work in my English articles at all. I feel so upset.

TUTOR: Just try your best to rewrite the draft.

STUDENT: I afraid I can't.

options.A: Provide visual aids, such as videos, computer programs, and pictures, for the English article draft.
options.B: Remind the student of the due date and stress the need to complete the draft to pass.
options.C: Tell the student directly, "Don't say you can't, you can," during the current review session.
options.D: Schedule frequent check-ins between sessions to discuss the details of the English article draft.
